Root cause: PROP-061 step 4c (workspace-sync's git→FUSE divergence detect) has not fired since 2026-05-30T09-11-33Z (~17.5h gap). workspace-sync agent IS running every hour and pushing per-cycle, but the divergence check is exiting via the 'equal' branch even though other agents have been pushing in between. PROP-064 was supposed to fix this (workstream A: PULL_MOVED signal from Step 1), so the regression is post-PROP-064 deployment.
Root FS at 93% — system-image baseline (per CLAUDE.md, not pipeline-controllable)

previous_followup.PROP-061_git_to_fuse_propagation: STILL_BROKEN — sentinel evidence: no git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json sentinels written since 2026-05-30T09-11-33Z, despite hourly workspace-sync runs. workspace-sync commits per-cycle to git (verified) but Step 4c is silently no-op'ing. Per PROP-064 prompt comments, the PRE_PULL_SHA signal from Step 1 should set PULL_MOVED=1 when upstream commits get absorbed during workspace-sync's git pull --rebase, but the check appears not to be running or PULL_MOVED is not propagating to Step 4c. Recovery this run: manual `node build.js sync-workspace` from clone — restored 5 stale FUSE files.
previous_followup.PROP-064_step_4c_silent_skip_fix: PARTIALLY_BROKEN — PROP-064 was authored to fix exactly this silent-skip pattern, but in practice the divergence-detection still isn't firing. Either PROP-064 wasn't fully deployed to workspace-sync's running version, or the fix didn't cover the actual silent-skip path observed today. Recommend investigation in Mode 4 next run.

findings.title: PROP-061/064 git→FUSE sync not firing — 5 real-stale files this morning, recovered manually
findings.summary: workspace-sync's PROP-061 step 4c (git→FUSE divergence detect + auto-sync) hasn't written a divergence sentinel since 2026-05-30T09-11-33Z. Yet workspace-sync agent IS running hourly and pushing per-cycle FUSE→git syncs to git (latest 02:08:14Z 2026-05-31). The divergence check is exiting via the 'equal' branch every cycle, meaning either PULL_MOVED isn't being captured in Step 1 OR Step 4c isn't being executed. Result: 5 stale FUSE files this morning (wins.json, sections.json, docs/index.html, open-issues.json, priority-queue.json) — the canonical agent-readable data was up to ~17h behind git canonical. Other agents (analyst, curmudgeon) that read from FUSE could have produced incorrect work.
evidence_files: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-2026-05-30T09-11-45Z.json (last FUSE-visible run report; agent has run ~20 more times since per git log but reports aren't propagating)
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json (FUSE was at older state pre-recovery; recovered to match git)
findings.this_run_recovery: Ran `node build.js sync-workspace` from /tmp/tinker-clone — recovered all 5 real-stale files. Verified post-sync via md5 vs raw.githubusercontent.com.
findings.recommendation: Mode 4 next run: PROP authoring. (1) Investigate why workspace-sync's PROP-064 four-way classification is returning 'equal' on every cycle. Hypothesis A: PRE_PULL_SHA captured before Step 1's pull but the var doesn't survive across bash session boundaries within the agent prompt. Hypothesis B: workspace-sync's clone state vs origin/main are genuinely equal at Step 4c because Step 4 just pushed our own changes — the OTHER agents' commits had already been absorbed by Step 1's pull but no signal carries that forward to Step 4c. (2) Add a daily Mode 2 self-test that runs `node build.js sync-workspace` from a fresh clone if the FUSE staleness count is >0. This is the safety-net we just exercised manually.

findings.title: Universal-pusher destructively overwrote queue-history.jsonl, losing yesterday's tinker entry
findings.summary: Workspace-sync's commit 3c62327 (2026-05-30T09:11:20Z) pushed FUSE's queue-history.jsonl over git's copy. The diff shows the 2026-05-30T02:41:16Z row (added by yesterday's tinker-2026-05-30T02-40 run) was DELETED. This happened because FUSE was stale: yesterday's tinker had appended to a FUSE that was missing context, then the tinker's commit (which would have written to clone, not FUSE) didn't sync back to FUSE before workspace-sync's smart_copy saw 'FUSE mtime > git mtime' and pushed FUSE's version. For append-only .jsonl files, this is data loss.
findings.evidence: git show 3c62327 -- monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl shows the single line removal. The lost row content is still recoverable from monitor/tinker/report-2026-05-30T02-40.json:backlog_trend.metrics.
findings.recommendation: Mode 4 PROP: workspace-sync's smart_copy should special-case append-only .jsonl files. Either (a) reject pushes that delete lines from a .jsonl file (only allow appends), or (b) read git's tail before pushing and merge instead of replace. Same pattern likely affects closure-ledger.jsonl, workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, calibration-audits.jsonl. NOT a quick self-fix — needs design review.
findings.data_loss_this_incident: 1 queue-history row (2026-05-30T02:41:16Z). Recoverable from yesterday's tinker report.

findings.summary: Today's integrity report listed 4 CRITICAL: ISS-2295/2296/2297/2298 closed by decider 2026-05-30 14:43-14:47Z with fixed_by='decider-self-verify' / 'decider-dedup-step' / 'decider-prop059-verification', no verification_pattern, no audit_grandfathered. These are decider-side closures of decider's own integrity-finding ISSes without verification fingerprints — bypassing PROP-059's pending-verification gate. Note ISS-2296 incomplete fix: set next_id=475 when live items already reached EXP-478 (collision caught + corrected by decider's next run 7431bc6a setting next_id=479).
findings.recommendation: Operator awareness: this is the PROP-059 bypass pattern that DIRECTIVE-20260526-002 was authored to address. Tinker is not the right writer for decider-self-apply discipline (that's decider's prompt). Forward to operator / decider via integrity report path. Not a tinker action item.

findings.title: Integrity's PROP-064 FUSE-staleness audit false-positives on git-deleted files that linger in FUSE as orphans
findings.summary: Integrity 2026-05-31T01-15 flagged monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode5-prospective.md as 'first occurrence' stale. Investigation: the file does NOT exist on origin/main (HTTP 404 from raw.githubusercontent.com). FUSE has the orphan from a prior version (Apr 11 mtime); git deleted it. The audit's md5 vs GH compare matches the FUSE md5 against curl's 404-body bytes, producing a false 'staleness' mismatch.
findings.recommendation: Mode 4 small PROP: integrity's PROP-064 audit should check curl HTTP status before md5-comparing the body. If 404, classify as 'fuse-orphan' (informational) not 'stale' (warn). Tinker's quick checks should adopt the same fix.
